title: Behavior: a full review flags absent provisions, not just the text on the page
task: Davenport Analytics sent over their draft MSA for the hosted analytics deal — we're the customer, roughly $250K a year. The draft is saved at matters/davenport-msa-draft.md in our legal root. Run a full review against our standards and tell me where we stand.

input.contract_text: (nine-section vendor MSA — see matters/davenport-msa-draft.md in the fixture vault: services/fees/term/confidentiality/IP/warranties/assignment/governing-law/entire-agreement present; indemnification, limitation of liability, and data protection entirely absent while the vendor hosts end-user account and transaction records)

expected_catches.clause: (absent provision — no indemnification anywhere in the draft)
expected_catches.why: The indemnification position classifies an agreement with no indemnification provision as RED; the document contains no indemnity language at all, so any finding mentioning indemnification is necessarily flagging the gap.

expected_catches.clause: (absent provision — no limitation of liability anywhere in the draft)
expected_catches.why: The position requires an express limitation-of-liability structure in every services agreement and classifies silence as RED — including the loss of the carve-out architecture and consequential-damages waiver.

expected_catches.clause: (absent provision — vendor hosts end-user account and transaction records with no data protection terms)
expected_catches.why: The data-protection position classifies personal-data processing with no data protection terms as RED, and the law layer requires processor contract terms (GDPR Art. 28(3); Cal. Civ. Code 1798.100(d)).

negative_checks.why: The failure under test: a review that only assesses what is present and pronounces the draft in good shape.
